docs.title: Hit Prompt Engine
docs.text:
A data pipeline that turns 50 years of Billboard chart history into Suno-optimized prompt packs — copyright-safe, DNA-driven, six variations per track.

No Suno API calls. This tool generates optimized prompts and manages track DNA. You paste the output into Suno Custom Mode.

docs.title: Why?
docs.text:
Suno Custom Mode requires two fields: a style prompt (200 chars) and lyrics. Writing these by hand for bulk creation is tedious and inconsistent. This engine:

  1. Ingests Billboard chart metadata (1976–present)
  2. Enriches it with MusicBrainz IDs / Spotify audio features
  3. Runs audio analysis via librosa + PANNs (BPM, key, structure, instrumentation)
  4. Compiles 6 copyright-safe variations per track
  5. Scores each pack with a Pat Pattison–inspired linter

docs.title: Quick links
docs.text:
• Getting Started
  • Architecture
  • API Reference
  • GitHub Repository

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text: Method Endpoint Description GET /health Service health check GET /tracks Search catalog (?q=&genre=&year_from=&year_to=) GET /tracks/{track_id} Full TrackDNA GET /prompts/{track_id} 6-variation prompt pack with lint scores POST /lint Lint any style+lyrics pair GET /stats Catalog summary

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
curl http://localhost:8000/prompts/abc123def456 | jq '.variations[0]'

{
  "id": "V1_faithful",
  "name": "Faithful Essence",
  "suno_style": "pop, 118 BPM, high energy, driving rhythm, C major, ...",
  "suno_lyrics": "(Theme direction: gravity pulling us together)\n[Verse]\n...",
  "instrumental": false,
  "lint": { "passed": true, "score": 0.92, "warnings": [], "errors": [] }
}

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
Billboard Hot-100 (1976–2026)
         │
         ▼
  ┌─────────────────────┐
  │  Phase 1: Ingestion  │  billboard_puller → deduper → MusicBrainz → Spotify
  └─────────────────────┘
         │
         ▼
  ┌─────────────────────┐
  │  Phase 2: Audio DNA  │  librosa (BPM/key/structure) + PANNs (instruments/mood)
  └─────────────────────┘
         │
         ▼
  ┌─────────────────────┐
  │  Prompt Compiler     │  style_builder + lyrics_builder + 6-variation engine
  └─────────────────────┘
         │
         ▼
  ┌─────────────────────┐
  │  FastAPI / Dagster   │  REST API + visual DAG orchestration
  └─────────────────────┘

docs.title: Module map
docs.text: Directory Role ingestion/ Billboard data pull, dedup (rapidfuzz), genre→chart mapping enrichment/ MusicBrainz IDs, Spotify audio features, genre normalization dna/ Audio analysis (librosa), PANNs inference, theme extraction prompt_compiler/ Style field builder, lyrics scaffolds, 6 variations, linter+scorer api/ FastAPI REST endpoints orchestration/ Dagster asset pipeline + weekly schedule config/ YAML: hit policy, genre taxonomy, variation recipes

docs.title: Environment
docs.text: Variable Required Default Description RAPIDAPI_KEY Yes — Billboard genre charts + Spotify proxy HIT_ENGINE_DB No hit_engine.db SQLite database path DATABASE_URL No — PostgreSQL connection string SPOTIFY_CLIENT_ID No — Native Spotify API fallback SPOTIFY_CLIENT_SECRET No — Native Spotify API fallback MUSICBRAINZ_CONTACT No — User-Agent email for MusicBrainz API

docs.title: YAML configs
docs.text: File Purpose config/hit_policy.yaml Eligibility: peak position, weeks on chart, chart list config/genre_taxonomy.yaml 15 canonical genres mapped to Suno tags config/variation_recipes.yaml 6 variation blueprints with BPM deltas

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: See CONTRIBUTING.md in the repository root for full instructions.

docs.title: Quick checklist
docs.text:
1. ruff check . && ruff format --check .
  2. pytest tests/
  3. bandit -r . --skip B101 -x tests/
  4. pip-audit -r requirements.txt

docs.title: Docker
docs.text:
# API only
docker compose up

# API + Dagster orchestration UI
docker compose --profile orchestration up

docs.title: GCP Cloud Run
docs.text:
gcloud builds submit --config cloudbuild.yaml

cloudbuild.yaml builds the Docker image, pushes to Artifact Registry, deploys the API service (hit-prompt-engine) and updates the weekly batch job (hit-engine-weekly-ingest).

docs.title: Manual
docs.text:
pip install -r requirements.txt
python run_pipeline.py serve --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Python 3.11+
  • API key for RapidAPI (Billboard + Spotify proxy)
  • Audio files (optional, for Phase 2)

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
pip install -r requirements.txt

For GPU-accelerated PANNs (~10× faster):

pip install torch torchaudio --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu121

docs.title: Configure
docs.text:
cp .env.example .env
# Edit .env with your RapidAPI key

docs.title: Run Phase 1 (no audio files needed)
docs.text:
python run_pipeline.py phase1 --start 2024-01-01 --end 2024-12-31

This pulls Billboard chart data, deduplicates tracks, and enriches with MusicBrainz and Spotify metadata.

docs.title: Run Phase 2 (requires audio files)
docs.text:
Drop {track_id}.mp3 files into audio_cache/, then:

python run_pipeline.py phase2 --limit 100

docs.title: Start the API
docs.text:
python run_pipeline.py serve
# → http://localhost:8000/docs
